one_time_description: Large one-time other revenue in 2021 ($21,521,438) likely related to land development or settlement proceeds; 2022 other revenue also elevated at $957,485 vs prior year normal levels
economic_notes: Nation operates forestry operations through Matsqui First Nation Development Limited Partnership and land development at 2179 McCallum Road through Matsqui Development Limited Partnership. Minor distributions paid to band members (summer $1,500 and winter $2,500 per minor). Ministry of Transportation settlement of $500,000 for Mt Lehman Interchange noted historically.
notes: Fiscal year is 2022, not 2024. Four GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: Matsqui Development Corporation Ltd., Matsqui First Nation Development Limited Partnership, Matsqui Development G.P. Ltd., and Matsqui Development Limited Partnership (forestry and land development). One consolidated subsidiary: 2179 McCallum Nominee Ltd. Minors Trust Funds ($2,596,412) classified as both a liability and offset by temporary investments. Prior year 2021 had anomalously large Other Revenue of $21,521,438 driving surplus of $21,782,025 — likely land sale or development proceeds. No schedule of remuneration provided. Health and Education reported as single segment; split not available. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $1,104,053 + Government of Canada $1,906,420 + CMHC $41,933 + Province of BC $271,074 + BC First Nation Gaming $179,779 + Other First Nation Government $192,797 = $3,696,056.
